title: Integrated Studies
subject: Integrated Studies
description: Learn about Jamaica, nature, health, and the world around you
curriculum: Jamaica NSC Grade 2 - Integrated Studies (Civics, Geography, History, Science, HFLE)
nscObjectives: Describe how the brain, heart and skeleton work together
Explain the importance of regular exercise in keeping bodies healthy
Identify the five senses and their functions
Practice healthy habits and personal hygiene
Identify national symbols of Jamaica and their significance
Identify national heroes and their contributions
Describe features of the local community
Identify community helpers and their roles
Describe weather patterns and seasons in Jamaica (wet and dry)

questions.prompt: What is the capital city of Jamaica?
questions.hint: This city is in the south-east of Jamaica.

questions.prompt: What are Jamaica's national colours?
questions.hint: Look at the Jamaican flag!

questions.prompt: What is Jamaica's national bird?
questions.hint: It's a beautiful hummingbird with long tail feathers!

questions.prompt: What is Jamaica's national fruit?
questions.hint: It's often eaten with saltfish for breakfast!

questions.prompt: What ocean surrounds Jamaica?
questions.hint: Jamaica is in the Caribbean!

sections.name: Living and Non-Living Things
sections.instruction: Living things grow, move, and need food and water.

questions.prompt: Which one is a LIVING thing?
questions.hint: Living things grow and need water!

questions.prompt: Which one is a NON-LIVING thing?
questions.hint: Non-living things don't grow or eat.

questions.prompt: Select ALL the LIVING things:
questions.hint: Living things need food and water to survive.

questions.prompt: What do ALL living things need?
questions.hint: Every plant and animal needs this to survive!

sections.name: My Body (Brain, Heart, Skeleton)
sections.instruction: NSC Theme: Who Am I? Learn about how your brain, heart and skeleton work together.

questions.prompt: Which part of your body helps you THINK and LEARN?
questions.hint: This is inside your head and controls everything you do!

questions.prompt: What does your HEART do?
questions.hint: Put your hand on your chest - feel it beating!

questions.prompt: What does your SKELETON do?
options: Gives your body shape and protects organs
questions.correct: Gives your body shape and protects organs
questions.hint: Your bones make up your skeleton!

questions.prompt: How many senses do we have?
questions.hint: Sight, hearing, smell, taste, and touch!

questions.prompt: Which body part do you use to SEE?
questions.hint: You use these to look at things!

questions.prompt: Which body part do you use to HEAR?
questions.hint: You use these to listen to music!

questions.prompt: The brain, heart and skeleton work TOGETHER to keep you:
questions.hint: They all have important jobs to keep your body working!

sections.name: Staying Healthy
sections.instruction: Good habits keep us healthy and strong!

questions.prompt: When should you wash your hands?
options: Before eating and after using the bathroom
questions.correct: Before eating and after using the bathroom
questions.hint: Washing hands stops germs from spreading!

questions.prompt: How many times a day should you brush your teeth?
questions.hint: Morning and night!

questions.prompt: Which food is HEALTHY for your body?
questions.hint: These foods have vitamins that help you grow!

questions.prompt: What helps your body stay strong?
questions.hint: Running, jumping, and playing sports keep you fit!

sections.name: Weather and Seasons
sections.instruction: Learn about different types of weather.

questions.prompt: What do we call water falling from the sky?
questions.hint: This makes puddles on the ground!

questions.prompt: What should you use on a sunny day to protect your skin?
questions.hint: This protects you from the hot sun!

questions.prompt: What should you carry when it might rain?
questions.hint: This keeps you dry in the rain!

questions.prompt: Jamaica has two main seasons. What are they?
questions.hint: We get more rain in one season than the other!
